title: OK Legislature Passes Two More Useless Pro-Life Bills
author: James Silberman
orientation: An abolitionist critique of two Oklahoma pro-life bills (SB918 and SB584) sent to Governor Stitt's desk in April 2021, arguing that a 'trigger' bill conditioned on the Supreme Court overturning Roe and a defunding bill targeting fetal body part traffickers are toothless gestures that perpetuate judicial supremacy and prop up the careers of the very Republicans who block abolition.

questions.question: What did the Oklahoma Legislature do during the week described in the article, and how many pro-life bills ended up on Governor Stitt's desk?
questions.answer: Earlier that week the Oklahoma Senate sent three pro-life House bills to the governor, and on Thursday the House approved two pro-life Senate bills, bringing the total sitting on Governor Kevin Stitt's desk to five.
questions.quote: On Thursday, the House approved two pro-life Senate Bills, making for a total of five pro-life bills sitting on Governor Kevin Stitt’s desk.

questions.question: What is SB918 and what conditions does it set before any pro-life regulations would actually be repealed?
questions.answer: SB918 is a 'trigger' bill that would only repeal Oklahoma's pro-life regulations if the U.S. Supreme Court overrules the central holding of Roe v. Wade (as reaffirmed by Casey) or if a constitutional amendment is adopted that restores the state's authority to prohibit abortion. In other words, it does nothing unless and until federal authorities grant permission.
questions.quote: Treat recognized the truth of what Silk was saying and amended SB195 (originally a different pro-life bill) to make it repeal all pro-life regulations in Oklahoma if “The United States Supreme Court overrules the central holding of Roe v. Wade…reaffirmed by Planned Parenthood of Southeastern Pennsylvania v. Casey…thereby restoring to the State of Oklahoma the authority to prohibit abortion” or “An amendment to the United States Constitution is adopted that restores to the State of Oklahoma the authority to prohibit abortion.”

questions.question: How did the partisan vote on SB918 break down, and what does Silberman say it reveals about Democrats in the legislature?
questions.answer: SB918 passed both chambers along party lines, with the lone Republican defection being abolitionist Senator Warren Hamilton. Every Democrat voted against it because, in Silberman's reading, they understand that pro-life regulations are precisely what would keep abortion legal if Roe were overturned, and they intend to defend those 'pro-life victories.'
questions.quote: Every Democrat in both chambers of the State legislature voted against the bill because they know that pro-life regulations are what would keep abortion legal if *Roe* is ever overturned. They will fight to preserve those “pro-life victories.”

questions.question: Why does the author call the trigger language in SB918 'insidious' rather than merely cautious?
questions.answer: Because the bill's wording presupposes that Oklahoma currently lacks the authority to prohibit abortion and is waiting on the Supreme Court to grant it. Silberman argues Oklahoma already has both the Biblical and Constitutional obligation to outlaw murder and provide equal protection right now, so SB918 entrenches a false hierarchy in which the Supreme Court stands above God and the Constitution.
questions.quote: SB918 perpetuates the fiction that the authority of the supreme court stands over the authority of God and Constitution.

questions.question: What scriptural and constitutional grounds does Silberman cite for Oklahoma's present obligation to abolish abortion?
questions.answer: He cites the prohibitions on murder in Exodus 20:13, Romans 13:3, and Genesis 9:6, alongside the Fifth Amendment's protection against deprivation of life, and grounds equal protection in Deuteronomy 1:17 and the Fourteenth Amendment, calling these non-negotiable responsibilities of Oklahoma's governing authorities.
questions.quote: Outlawing murder (Exodus 20:13, Romans 13:3, Genesis 9:6) (U.S. Const. amend. V) and establishing equal protection of the law for all (Deuteronomy 1:17) (U.S. Const. amend. XIV) are non-negotiable responsibilities of Oklahoma’s governing authorities.

questions.question: What does SB584 actually do, and why does the author argue it is functionally useless?
questions.answer: SB584 amends Oklahoma's 2018 Defunding Fetal Body Parts Trafficking Act to prohibit subdivisions of the state from giving funds to businesses convicted in court of trafficking fetal body parts. Because no court ever actually convicted Planned Parenthood despite the undercover footage, the underlying statute does not deny Planned Parenthood any funding in Oklahoma, making SB584 (and its predecessor) merely symbolic.
questions.quote: This means that the Defunding Fetal Body Parts Trafficking Act does not deny funding to Planned Parenthood in Oklahoma. SB584 and its 2018 predecessor are merely for show.

questions.question: According to the article, who is actually keeping abortion legal in Oklahoma?
questions.answer: Not Planned Parenthood, but pro-life Republican politicians themselves. Even if defunding bills hurt Planned Parenthood's bottom line, it would not change abortion's legal status, because the GOP officeholders who pass these incremental measures are the ones blocking actual abolition.
questions.quote: Planned Parenthood is not keeping abortion legal in Oklahoma. Pro-life Republican politicians are.

questions.question: What strategic role does Silberman say 'useless' pro-life bills play for the pro-life establishment?
questions.answer: He argues these bills are deployed as the excuse not to abolish abortion and as career insurance for the politicians blocking abolition. The pro-life establishment's strategy is to kill the abolition bill while passing enough symbolic pro-life legislation to keep their credibility with Christian voters intact.
questions.quote: The strategy of the pro-life establishment is to kill the bill to abolish abortion, but pass enough of these useless pro-life bills to regain their credibility with Christian voters.

questions.question: How does the article frame the historical exchange between Greg Treat and former Senator Joseph Silk that gave rise to SB918's repeal language?
questions.answer: In 2019, while discussing the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act (SB13), Silk explained to Senate Pro Tem Greg Treat that abolishing abortion requires repealing the abortion-allowing regulations passed by pro-lifers. Treat acknowledged the point and amended SB195 to repeal those regulations, but only contingent on federal action, an approach he later refiled as SB918.
questions.quote: Silk explained that to abolish abortion, abortion-allowing regulations (including those passed by pro-lifers) have to be done away with.

questions.question: What does Silberman say will finally force pro-life politicians to abolish abortion?
questions.answer: He predicts that when enough Christian voters see through the establishment's charade and reject incremental schemes and symbolic pro-life bills, the politicians will lose the cover those bills provide and have no remaining option but to abolish abortion outright.
questions.quote: When enough of us see through their charade and reject their incremental schemes and pro-life bills, they’ll have no choice but to abolish abortion.

related.title: OK Legislature Sends 3 Useless Pro-Life Bills to Governor Stitt
related.reason: Directly linked from this article as the companion piece covering the three House bills sent to Stitt earlier in the same week.

related.title: The Three Pro-Life Men Keeping Abortion Legal in Oklahoma
related.reason: Develops the article's central claim that Oklahoma's pro-life Republican leadership, not Planned Parenthood, is what keeps abortion legal in the state.

related.title: The Inexhaustible Cowardice of Oklahoma Senate President Greg Treat
related.reason: Profiles Greg Treat, the Senate Pro Tem who authored SB918 and the trigger language critiqued here.

related.title: Five Reasons Abolitionists Oppose Incremental Pro-Life Bills
related.reason: Provides the broader abolitionist framework behind this article's argument that 'useless' pro-life bills actively prop up the abortion regime.

related.title: Kevin Stitt Makes Pro-Life Gesture With California Travel Ban
related.reason: Earlier critique of Governor Stitt, the executive whose desk is now stacked with the five symbolic bills discussed here.

related.title: June v. Russo Must Be the Death Knell of Pro-Life Judicial Supremacy and Incrementalism
related.reason: Expands on the 'judicial supremacy' charge SB918 embodies by conditioning state action on the Supreme Court overturning Roe.

related.title: Watch of Giants Heroes: Joseph Silk
related.reason: Background on the abolitionist former senator whose 2019 conversation with Treat is recounted as the origin of SB918's repeal language.
